[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [33°53′07″S 151°12′57″E﻿ / ﻿33.88537°S 151.21587°E﻿ / -33.88537; 151.21587](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hopetoun_Hotel&params=33.88537_S_151.21587_E_type:landmark)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Historic Sydney hotel and music venue

Hopetoun Hotel

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| The Hoey | |
| The disused Hopetoun Hotel in 2020 | |
| Map Interactive map of Hopetoun Hotel | |
| Former names | * The Cockatoo Inn (1839–1861) * The Sportsman’s Arms (1861–1873) * Kilkenny Inn (1873–1885) * The Great Western Hotel (1885–1901)) |
| Address | 416 Bourke Street, [Surry Hills](/wiki/Surry_Hills "Surry Hills") [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") Australia |
| Coordinates | [33°53′07″S 151°12′57″E﻿ / ﻿33.88537°S 151.21587°E﻿ / -33.88537; 151.21587](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hopetoun_Hotel&params=33.88537_S_151.21587_E_type:landmark) |
| Owner | Evangelos and Anastasia Patakas |
| Type | Music venue |
| Designation | Heritage listed |
| Construction | |
| Built | 1836~1839 |
| Closed | 2009 |

The **Hopetoun Hotel**, colloquially referred to as **The Hoey**,[[1]](#cite_note-legacy_and_the_legend-1) is a historic [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") hotel and live music venue in [Surry Hills](/wiki/Surry_Hills "Surry Hills"). It was built somewhere between 1836 and 1839, originally under the name of the Cookatoo Inn and then in 1901 revamped and named in honour of the first Governor General, [Lord Hopetoun](/wiki/Lord_Hopetoun "Lord Hopetoun").[[2]](#cite_note-SHR-2) In 1997 it was purchased by siblings Evangelos and Anastasia Patakas for $1.5 million and became a live music venue that saw performances by bands including the [Hoodoo Gurus](/wiki/Hoodoo_Gurus "Hoodoo Gurus") and [Wolfmother](/wiki/Wolfmother "Wolfmother").[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 2009, it was closed due to accumulated fines and police citations for noise violations in a residential area.[[4]](#cite_note-4) It has remained shut since, though it was used in 2012 as a set for [The Wolverine (film)](/wiki/The_Wolverine_(film) "The Wolverine (film)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

It was a much loved venue by the community and [Sarah Blasko](/wiki/Sarah_Blasko "Sarah Blasko") spoke of its unique nature, "It's so intimate, you almost feel like you could order a drink from the stage".[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hopetoun_Hotel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The hotel is a heritage listed building[[7]](#cite_note-7) and has occupied the site for over 150 years forming a landmark feature in Surry Hills. It is an important architectural example of an early Federation warehouse style face brick building.[[2]](#cite_note-SHR-2)[[8]](#cite_note-colonial_survivor-8)

### Renaming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hopetoun_Hotel&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Renaming")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/%281%29_Hopetoun_Hotel.JPG/250px-%281%29_Hopetoun_Hotel.JPG)](/wiki/File:(1)_Hopetoun_Hotel.JPG)

The hotel in 2010

The hotel has been renamed many times:[[8]](#cite_note-colonial_survivor-8)

* 1839–1861: The Cockatoo Inn
* 1861–1873: The Sportsman's Arms
* 1873–1885: Kilkenny Inn
* 1885–1901: The Great Western Hotel
* 1901–present: Hopetoun Hotel

## Notable performances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hopetoun_Hotel&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notable performances")]

Acts who have played at the hotel include:[[1]](#cite_note-legacy_and_the_legend-1)[[9]](#cite_note-setlists-9)

* [Ed Kuepper](/wiki/Ed_Kuepper "Ed Kuepper")
* [Kim Salmon](/wiki/Kim_Salmon "Kim Salmon")
* [Died Pretty](/wiki/Died_Pretty "Died Pretty")
* [Hoodoo Gurus](/wiki/Hoodoo_Gurus "Hoodoo Gurus") under the alias Dork Stick
* [Wolfmother](/wiki/Wolfmother "Wolfmother")
* [The Chemical Brothers](/wiki/The_Chemical_Brothers "The Chemical Brothers")
* [Luke Vibert](/wiki/Luke_Vibert "Luke Vibert")
* [Jan Jelinek](/wiki/Jan_Jelinek "Jan Jelinek")
* [Sarah Blasko](/wiki/Sarah_Blasko "Sarah Blasko")
* [Paul Kelly](/wiki/Paul_Kelly_(Australian_musician) "Paul Kelly (Australian musician)")
* [You Am I](/wiki/You_Am_I "You Am I") as the Question Fruit
* [Cockroaches](/wiki/The_Cockroaches "The Cockroaches")
* [The Coloured Girls](/wiki/The_Coloured_Girls "The Coloured Girls")
* [Michael Hutchence](/wiki/Michael_Hutchence "Michael Hutchence") ([INXS](/wiki/INXS "INXS"))
* [Angry Anderson](/wiki/Angry_Anderson "Angry Anderson")
* [Jenny Morris](/wiki/Jenny_Morris_(musician) "Jenny Morris (musician)")
* [Tim Freedman](/wiki/Tim_Freedman "Tim Freedman") (from [The Whitlams](/wiki/The_Whitlams "The Whitlams"))
* [Mental as Anything](/wiki/Mental_as_Anything "Mental as Anything")
* [Ratcat](/wiki/Ratcat "Ratcat")
* [The Watchmen](/wiki/The_Watchmen_(band) "The Watchmen (band)")
* [The Clouds](/wiki/The_Clouds_(Australian_band) "The Clouds (Australian band)")
* [The Grates](/wiki/The_Grates "The Grates")
* [The Jezabels](/wiki/The_Jezabels "The Jezabels")[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* [Xiu Xiu](/wiki/Xiu_Xiu "Xiu Xiu")
* [Because of Ghosts](/wiki/Because_of_Ghosts "Because of Ghosts")
* [Urthboy](/wiki/Urthboy "Urthboy")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* [David McCormack](/wiki/David_McCormack "David McCormack") and the Polaroids[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* [Smog](/wiki/Smog_(band) "Smog (band)")
* [Feist](/wiki/Feist_(singer) "Feist (singer)")
* [Gomez](/wiki/Gomez_(band) "Gomez (band)")
* [Spiderbait](/wiki/Spiderbait "Spiderbait")
* [Powderfinger](/wiki/Powderfinger "Powderfinger")
* [The Whitlams](/wiki/The_Whitlams "The Whitlams")
* [Paul Dempsey](/wiki/Paul_Dempsey "Paul Dempsey")
* [Cat Power](/wiki/Cat_Power "Cat Power")
* [Augie March](/wiki/Augie_March "Augie March")
* [Machine Gun Fellatio](/wiki/Machine_Gun_Fellatio "Machine Gun Fellatio")
* [Gersey](/wiki/Gersey "Gersey")
* [New Buffalo](/wiki/Sally_Seltmann "Sally Seltmann")
* [Dappled Cities](/wiki/Dappled_Cities "Dappled Cities")
* [Decoder Ring](/wiki/Decoder_Ring "Decoder Ring")
* [Architecture in Helsinki](/wiki/Architecture_in_Helsinki "Architecture in Helsinki")
* [The Mountain Goats](/wiki/The_Mountain_Goats "The Mountain Goats")
